question.text: An index fund has a Total Expense Ratio (TER) of 0.5% per year. If Mr. Reddy invests Rs. 1,00,000 in this fund, approximately how much will he pay as expenses in one year?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** TER (Total Expense Ratio) is the annual cost charged by the fund. If TER is 0.5%, then on an investment of Rs. 1,00,000: Expense = 1,00,000 x 0.5/100 = Rs. 500 per year. This amount is deducted from the fund's NAV and is not charged separately. **The Trap:** Rs. 5,000 would be 5% — that is 10 times the actual TER. Rs. 50 would be 0.05%. Always be careful with decimal calculations. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under fund expenses. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When explaining TER to clients, use rupee amounts instead of percentages. Saying "you pay Rs. 500 per year on Rs. 1 lakh" is much clearer than saying "0.5% TER" — especially for clients who are not finance professionals.
